title: ruellia terminalis var. grandiflora
intro: Ruellia terminalis var. grandiflora, also known as the 'Grandiflora Ruellia' or 'Butterfly Blue', is a flowering plant species in the Acanthaceae family, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ruellia terminalis var. grandiflora include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for anxiety and stress relief. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
